root_text.an6.21:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati sāmagāmake pokkharaṇiyāyaṁ.
root_text.an6.21:1.2: Atha kho aññatarā devatā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ pokkharaṇiyaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho sā devatā bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.21:2.4: ime kho, bhante, tayo dhammā bhikkhuno parihānāya saṁvattantī”ti.
root_text.an6.21:2.7: Atha kho sā devatā “samanuñño me satthā”ti bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā tatthevantaradhāyi.
root_text.an6.21:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā tassā rattiyā accayena bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an6.21:3.2: “imaṁ, bhikkhave, rattiṁ aññatarā devatā abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ pokkharaṇiyaṁ obhāsetvā yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho, bhikkhave, sā devatā maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.21:3.6: ime kho, bhante, tayo dhammā bhikkhuno parihānāya saṁvattantī’ti.
root_text.an6.21:3.8: Idaṁ vatvā maṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā tatthevantaradhāyi.
root_text.an6.21:3.9: Tesaṁ vo, bhikkhave, alābhā tesaṁ dulladdhaṁ, ye vo devatāpi jānanti kusalehi dhammehi parihāyamāne.
root_text.an6.21:4.1: Aparepi, bhikkhave, tayo parihāniye dhamme desessāmi.
root_text.an6.21:4.2: Taṁ suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha, bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an6.21:4.3: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an6.21:4.7: ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo parihāniyā dhammā.
root_text.an6.21:5.1: Ye hi keci, bhikkhave, atītamaddhānaṁ parihāyiṁsu kusalehi dhammehi, sabbete imeheva chahi dhammehi parihāyiṁsu kusalehi dhammehi.
root_text.an6.21:5.2: Yepi hi keci, bhikkhave, anāgatamaddhānaṁ parihāyissanti kusalehi dhammehi, sabbete imeheva chahi dhammehi parihāyissanti kusalehi dhammehi.
root_text.an6.21:5.3: Yepi hi keci, bhikkhave, etarahi parihāyanti kusalehi dhammehi, sabbete imeheva chahi dhammehi parihāyanti kusalehi dhammehī”ti.

translation_text.an6.21:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans near the little village of Sāma, by a lotus pond.
translation_text.an6.21:1.2: Then, late at night, a glorious deity, lighting up the entire lotus pond, went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an6.21:2.1: “Sir, three qualities lead to the decline of a mendicant.
translation_text.an6.21:2.4: These three qualities lead to the decline of a mendicant.”
translation_text.an6.21:2.7: Then that deity, knowing that the teacher approved, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on his right, before vanishing right there.
translation_text.an6.21:3.1: Then, when the night had passed, the Buddha told the mendicants all that had happened, adding:
translation_text.an6.21:3.9: “It’s unfortunate for those of you who even the deities know are declining in skillful qualities.
translation_text.an6.21:4.1: I will teach you three more qualities that lead to decline.
translation_text.an6.21:4.2: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an6.21:4.5: “And what, mendicants, are three qualities that lead to decline?
translation_text.an6.21:4.6: Enjoyment of company, being hard to admonish, and having bad friends.
translation_text.an6.21:4.7: These three qualities lead to decline.
translation_text.an6.21:5.1: Whether in the past, future, or present, all those who decline in skillful qualities do so because of these six qualities.”

reference_text.an6.21:1.1: bj21.50, csp1ed16.273, csp2ed16.273, ms16A6_133, msdiv21, pts-vp-pli3.309, vri38.28
